stories.headline: Vance says US, Iran have made “a lot of progress” towards deal
stories.summary: US Vice President JD Vance says Washington and Tehran have made “a lot of progress” towards ceasefire

stories.headline: Trump pledges ‘final determination’ on Iran deal to extend ceasefire
stories.summary: US president seeks agreement on ‘unrestricted traffic’ in Strait of Hormuz

stories.headline: Netanyahu says he has directed IDF to increase control of Gaza to 70%
stories.summary: The expansion in control by Israel would contradict the terms of the ceasefire  Israel and Hamas agreed to in October 2025.

stories.headline: Sudan medical group says RSF-affiliated fighters kill 27 civilians
stories.summary: The attacks come as nearly 19.5 million Sudanese face severe hunger amid the country’s deepening humanitarian crisis.

stories.headline: Russian drone hits apartment building in Romania
stories.summary: Nato and Bucharest condemn ‘reckless’ escalation after two people injured in Galaţi city

stories.headline: Trump says ‘final determination’ to be made on possible Iran deal
stories.summary: Deep mistrust remains between Washington and Tehran as Iran's top negotiator urges action, not words.

stories.headline: Friday briefing: ​What do the cuts in aid mean for the fight against Ebola in the DRC?
stories.summary: In today’s newsletter: As the virus spreads across borders, health workers warn that weakened global support is making a prolonged crisis more likelyEbola is spreading rapidly in parts of east Africa. The deadly disease, which kills around half of those it infects, is suspected to have claimed the lives of at least 240 people since the outbreak began in Ituri province in the Democratic Republic of the Congo earlier this month.Public health offici

stories.headline: Nato and EU condemn Russia after drone hits Romanian residential block
stories.summary: Romania says the Russian drone was likely hit over Ukraine by its air defences and altered its trajectory.

stories.headline: 'Controversial' North Korean invasion setting for next Call of Duty game
stories.summary: Developer Infinity Ward said the game will be "grounded in the military authenticity" the series is known for.

stories.headline: BoE governor warns Middle East ceasefire would ‘still create uncertainty’
stories.summary: Andrew Bailey says rate cuts can only come when policymakers are ‘much more confident’

stories.headline: Nato says drone that crashed in Romania was ‘of Russian origin’ despite Moscow’s denials – as it happened
stories.summary: This blog is now closed, you can read more on this story hereThe incident comes just days after the Czech president, Petr Pavel, has urged Nato to “show its teeth” in response to Russia’s repeated testing of the alliance’s resolve on its eastern flank, suggesting a range of options including switching off its internet, cutting off its banks from global financial systems and shooting down jets that violate allied airspace.Speaking to the Guardian

stories.headline: Nato ready to defend ‘every inch’ of territory as Russian drone hits Romania
stories.summary: Mark Rutte says Moscow’s ‘reckless behaviour is danger to us all’ after drone hits apartment building, while Russia denies involvementThe Nato secretary general, Mark Rutte, has said the alliance is “ready to defend every inch” of its territory after a Russian drone hit an apartment building in Romania, a member state, during an overnight attack on neighbouring Ukraine.“Russia’s reckless behaviour is a danger to us all,” Rutte wrote on social med

stories.headline: EU to release billions in frozen funds for Hungary amid Magyar reforms
stories.summary: PM Peter Magyar called ​the agreement to unlock funds frozen under former leader Orban a 'historic' breakthrough.

stories.headline: France launches an inquiry into the treatment of activists on the Gaza flotilla
stories.summary: The French Foreign Minister, Jean-Noël Barrot, announced on Friday that he had referred the matter to the courts following reports of humiliation, rape and acts of torture against activists on board the Gaza flotilla.

stories.headline: What we know about the Geran-2 drone that hit a residential building in Romania
stories.summary: The Iranian-made Shahed drones are one of Russia's main long-range strike UAV’s used by Moscow against Ukraine. First deployed back in 2022, Russia then set up domestic production, calling them Geran.

stories.headline: TotalEnergies’ Oil Trading Reaps $2 Billion Per Year, CEO Says
stories.summary: TotalEnergies SE’s oil trading activities generally earn the company about $2 billion per year, Chief Executive Officer Patrick Pouyanné said, a rare comment on the vast size of the oil trading operations of some of Europe’s majors.

stories.headline: Deutsche Bank Raises 10-Year Treasury Yield Forecast on Fed View
stories.summary: Interest-rate strategists at Deutsche Bank AG boosted their forecast for the 10-year Treasury yield into the end of the year on the expectation that Federal Reserve officials now led by Chairman Kevin Warsh are finished cutting interest rates.

stories.headline: Lula Rejects US Terrorism Designation for Brazil Drug Gangs
stories.summary: President Luiz Inácio Lula da Silva blasted a US decision to label two major Brazilian criminal groups as terrorist organizations, calling it a threat to the nation’s sovereignty that won’t help its fight against drug trafficking.

stories.headline: Federal Judge Bars Trump From Immediately Setting Up $1.8 Billion Fund
stories.summary: A judge in Virginia temporarily blocked the Trump administration from transferring money in or out of the fund until the court could hear arguments in June.

stories.headline: Lula says Brazil will not be treated like ‘tinpot country’ after US designates gangs as terrorists
stories.summary: Marco Rubio made announcement after meeting president’s far-right challenger Flávio BolsonaroBrazil will not be treated as a “tinpot country,” the country’s president, Luiz Inácio da Silva, said on Friday after the United States designated Brazil’s two largest criminal gangs, the First Capital Command (PCC) and the Red Command, as foreign terrorist organisations.The announcement, made by Marco Rubio, the secretary of state, on Thursday, is being

stories.headline: Can the second coming of Tony Abbott resurrect the Liberal party? Or is it another step towards ‘self-destruction’
stories.summary: Angus Taylor believes the former PM is uniquely placed to help the party as its new president, but some fear he will render it even more unelectableGet our breaking news email, free app or daily news podcastImmediately after ousting Sussan Ley, Angus Taylor used his first press conference as opposition leader to debut a slogan he hoped would define a new era of Liberal conservatism.“The door must be shut,” Taylor said of his approach to prospecti

stories.headline: EU to release €16bn to Hungary previously frozen under Orbán
stories.summary: Decision comes as police announce policy U-turn to allow Pride parade to take place in BudapestThe EU is to release more than €16bn to Hungary that had been frozen under the rule of Viktor Orbán, with Ursula von der Leyen hailing the “winds of change” in the country since the election of Péter Magyar last month.The decision, described as a “historic breakthrough” by the new prime minister, comes as police in Hungary have said they will allow next

stories.headline: Editor's Choice: When sanctions drive innovation, Huawei's 'Her's Law' challenge
EU taxes on digital services, gambling, crypto could yield up to €11 billion per year – Commission
stories.summary: European Commission is examining proposals to finance the next EU budget through tax changes targeting digital services, gambling and crypto assets.

stories.headline: Hungary unlocks €16.4bn in EU funds after Magyar secures deal with Brussels
stories.summary: Hungarian Prime Minister Péter Magyar reached an agreement with the European Commission to unblock nearly all recovery and cohesion funds that had been frozen under his predecessor, Viktor Orbán.

stories.headline: Former US attorney general Pam Bondi defends her handling of Epstein files in congressional probe
stories.summary: The testimony comes about a month after America's top prosecutor was ousted by US President Donald Trump.

stories.headline: Ex-head monk of China's 'kung fu temple' jailed for embezzlement
stories.summary: Shi Yongxin is sentenced to 24 years in prison for embezzlement and bribery.

stories.headline: How China is breaking apart a people and its culture
stories.summary: The campaign of repression against the Uyghurs has entered a new phase

stories.headline: BOE's Bailey Sees 'Signs of Strains' in Private Credit
stories.summary: Bank of England Governor Andrew Bailey comments on the recent concerns about the private credit market during an interview with Bloomberg's Stephanie Flanders at a central banking conference in Iceland. (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: CFTC Paves Way for Crypto’s Trillion-Dollar Offshore Trade
stories.summary: For years, the most popular way to trade crypto — a never-expiring, highly leveraged contract called a perpetual future — has existed almost entirely beyond the reach of US regulators, thriving on offshore exchanges that operate outside American law.

stories.headline: Rio Tinto’s Aluminum Exports to US Rebound to Pretariff Levels
stories.summary: One of the world’s largest aluminum producers, Rio Tinto Group, says its shipments of the lightweight metal to the US have bounced back to levels from before President Donald Trump’s tariff offensive.

stories.headline: US Stocks Extend Rally on Peace Deal Hopes, AI Spending Optimism
stories.summary: US stocks rose on Friday, boosted by hopes of a peace deal between the US and Iran and expectations that spending on artificial intelligence will drive further gains in corporate earnings.

stories.headline: Gap CEO Remains Bullish Despite Lowering Sales Outlook
stories.summary:
Gap Inc. CEO Richard Dickson says the retailer delivered progress in the quarter, even after lowering its sales outlook. He says three out of their four brands are growing and they're raising the earnings outlook. Dickson speaks to Bloomberg's Lisa Abramowicz.

 (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: A City-Backed Grocery Store in Atlanta Offers a Model for Mamdani
stories.summary:
Food prices have become the animating issue in one of New York City Mayor Zohran Mamdani’s signature policies: a plan to open city-owned grocery stores.

Azalea Fresh Market's CEO Paul Nair joins Max Chafkin and Stacey Vanek Smith on the Everybody's Business podcast to discuss the popular city-backed grocery store in Atlanta and how it's serving as a model for Mamdani’s grocery store plan.  (Source: Bloomberg)

stories.headline: Why Hasn’t California Elected a Woman Governor?
stories.summary: Most states have had a woman serve as governor, but California is in the minority despite its progressive-minded electorate. Women who have reached the top elsewhere saw familiar roadblocks.

stories.headline: 3 Dead After Large Fire at Dallas Apartment Building
stories.summary: A damaged gas line led to a five-alarm fire in the Oak Cliff neighborhood. At least four were injured, and firefighters believe they may find more victims.

stories.headline: Canadian man admits sending ‘suicide packets’ to hundreds of people around world
stories.summary: Kenneth Law, who sold lethal chemicals online with instructions on how to use them, admits counselling or aiding suicide ‘It was too easy’: families ask how Kenneth Law enabled so many suicidesA Canadian man who mailed “suicide packets” of poison to more than 100 people in dozens of countries – including Canada, the UK, the US, Italy, Australia and New Zealand – has pleaded guilty to 14 counts of assisting suicide.Kenneth Law appeared in a packed

stories.headline: Colombia’s ‘Total Peace’ plan: A failure or unfinished business for Petro?
stories.summary: As Colombia prepares to elect a new president, candidates are debating the legacy of Gustavo Petro's signature policy.

stories.headline: JTB eyes return to profit growth on solid outbound, inbound travel
Japan passes tougher foreign investment law, paving way for CFIUS-like panel
Japanese and South Korean stock markets soar on anticipation of Iran deal
BYD's new 4-nm self-driving chip fails to dispel investors' growth concerns
Hong Kong and Singapore brace for AI chill in finance job market
Four EU countries push Brussels to ease carbon market pressure on industry
stories.summary: Estonia, France, Germany and Spain support the Emissions Trading System, the EU’s major climate policy tool, but want clearer, simpler and possibly more flexible rules to protect industrial competitiveness, according to a document seen by Euronews.

stories.headline: EU's six largest economies push for capital markets union
stories.summary: The EU’s six largest economies have reached a common agreement on how to integrate capital markets in an attempt to get a deal through a politically stagnant Brussels.

stories.headline: Chevron moves into Greek offshore gas exploration as Europe's energy focus shifts east
stories.summary: Chevron has applied to join a Greek offshore exploration area known as Block 10 in the Ionian Sea, in a move that could strengthen Greece's role in Eastern Mediterranean energy security.

stories.headline: Gaza City hospitals say several killed in strike, as Israel targets Hamas leaders
stories.summary: At least five children were reportedly killed in the strike, which appears to have targeted a Hamas commander.

stories.headline: Iran says it targeted American base after fresh US strikes
stories.summary: The hostilities come during a fragile ceasefire between the US and Iran, and protracted negotiations to end the three-month war.

stories.headline: Gold Jumps Most in Three Weeks as Trump Eyes ‘Final’ Iran Call
stories.summary: Gold jumped the most in more than three weeks after US President Donald Trump said he’s making a “final determination” on a preliminary deal to extend a ceasefire with Iran.

stories.headline: After stern words from Trump, U.S. and Iran trade strikes
stories.summary: The renewed violence came after the president said Iran was wrong if it thought he would feel pressure to make concessions and resolve the standoff before U.S. midterm elections later this year.

stories.headline: Learning from Ukraine war, Hezbollah is now using fibre-optic drones to hit Israel
stories.summary: Fibre-optic drones are now Hezbollah's primary weapon against Israel's soldiers and civilians.

stories.headline: How Cuba is addressing its housing crisis with shipping containers
stories.summary: BBC's Will Grant visited Barrio Toledo, where at least 700 containers are being repurposed into two-bedroom homes with kitchens, bathrooms and patios.

stories.headline: Michigan’s Whitmer walks back comment saying she won’t run for president in 2028
stories.summary: Whitmer, term-limited after two gubernatorial wins in her battleground state, has been widely viewed as a potential Democratic candidate.

stories.headline: What are Republicans going to campaign on?
stories.summary: Trump is unpopular. Gas prices are high. Republicans are searching for a midterm message.

stories.headline: Image of Thai police in sparkly dresses with handcuffed suspect turns out to be AI fake
stories.summary: Picture was created by administrator in charge of station’s Facebook account who wanted to create ‘friendlier image’It was an arresting image and an irresistible story. A group of tough Thai police officers – five men and one woman – all wearing elaborate festival-style dresses, surrounding a drug dealer they had caught while undercover.The image, released by local police, was so compelling that it found its way on to the front page of the UK’s D

stories.headline: Israeli strike in Gaza City kills new head of Hamas's military wing
stories.summary: Hamas says Mohammed Odeh was killed alongside his wife and two children in a strike on a residential building.

stories.headline: Trump says US 'not satisfied' with Iran deal yet
stories.summary: The US president says he believes Iran wants to reach a deal but there is still no agreement on its terms.

stories.headline: We analysed thousands of Trump's posts - here's what we found
stories.summary: The BBC's Ros Atkins and BBC Verify sifted through thousands of the president's posts.

stories.headline: Spanish police raid HQ of governing Socialists as corruption probe escalates
stories.summary: The searches come amid a series of scandals affecting Prime Minister Pedro Sánchez's PSOE.

stories.headline: South Korea detains dissident who fled China in rubber boat
stories.summary: Dong Guangping, an activist and former police officer, spent hours at sea before reaching Korean waters.

stories.headline: The world's carmakers are struggling to compete with China
stories.summary: The BBC visited China’s EV factories and found they are dominating the ecosystems shaping the global auto industry.

stories.headline: Iran condemns US strikes as 'gross violation' of ceasefire
stories.summary: The attacks took place while Iranian and Qatari negotiators were in Doha for peace talks.

stories.headline: China executes man for poisoning billionaire gaming tycoon
stories.summary: The court said the man poisoned the tycoon for sidelining him after he helped secure a deal with Netflix for the 3 Body Problem.

stories.headline: Trump tells US negotiators 'not to rush' into deal with Iran
stories.summary: The deal under discussion would involve a 60-day ceasefire extension during which the Strait of Hormuz would be reopened, according to US media.

stories.headline: More than 1.5m foreign pilgrims begin Hajj despite Iran war fears
stories.summary: Saudi Arabia says that is 11,000 more than the number of Muslims who travelled to the Gulf kingdom last year.

stories.headline: Anger grows after China's deadliest coal mining disaster in years
stories.summary: On China's tightly-controlled internet, people are calling for justice and questioning how this happened.

stories.headline: Bolivian minister's convoy ambushed while overseeing roadblock clearance
stories.summary: Security forces were met by resistance while attempting to clear highway barriers, amid unrest over the government's economic policies.

stories.headline: Gaza flotilla activists allege abuse by Israeli forces while detained
stories.summary: The detainees say they were slapped, hit, humiliated and even subjected to sexual violence, but Israel's prison service says the allegations are false.

stories.headline: I survived a missile strike in the Strait of Hormuz, but my friend has not been found
stories.summary: Sunil Puniya was on his first sea job when a missile struck the oil tanker Skylight at the start of the Iran war.

stories.headline: 'No means no': Greenlanders protest against Trump outside new US consulate
stories.summary: The inauguration of the new consulate comes amid a push by the US president for greater control over the island.
